description: Fix CLI UX friction: plugin search shows dangling separator when no description, and browser-tool config accepts any name without offline validation. LOW issues documented: status --json offline and help text inconsistency are already fixed in source.

userStories.title: Omit em-dash separator in plugin search when description is empty
userStories.description: As a user running opentabs plugin search, I want plugins with no description to display cleanly without a dangling em-dash separator so that the output looks polished.
acceptanceCriteria: When a plugin has no description (empty string or undefined), the search output omits the '—' separator entirely, producing: [label] name version by author
When a plugin has a description, the output remains: [label] name version — description by author
userStories.notes: File: platform/cli/src/commands/plugin.ts, line 424. The current template literal always includes '— ${desc}' even when desc is empty string (set at line 421). Fix by conditionally including the separator: use `${desc ? ` — ${desc}` : ''}` in the template at line 424. This is a one-line change.

userStories.title: Warn when browser-tool config set cannot validate tool name offline
userStories.description: As a user configuring browser tools while the server is offline, I want a clear warning that the tool name could not be validated so that I know to verify it when the server starts.
acceptanceCriteria: When server is running: existing validation warning behavior is preserved (warns if tool name doesn't match registered tools)
When server is NOT running: after writing the config, a warning message appears stating the tool name could not be validated because the server is not running
The warning suggests the user verify the tool name by running 'opentabs status' or checking 'opentabs config set browser-tool.' when the server is running
The config write still succeeds (forward compatibility preserved)
userStories.notes: File: platform/cli/src/commands/config.ts, function handleSetBrowserTool (line 330). Currently the function calls fetchToolNames(port) which returns null when server is unreachable, and the validation block at lines after 358 only warns when registeredTools is non-null. When registeredTools is null (server offline), add a dim warning like: 'Note: Could not validate tool name (server not running). Verify with: opentabs config set browser-tool.'. The same pattern could be applied to handleSetTool (line 287) for plugin tools, though plugin tool validation already has the same null-check gap. Keep the fix small — just add the offline warning message in the null case for both handlers.
